articles.title: Judge Temporarily Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
articles.description: The delay is intended to give the court time to consider a lawsuit that argues the $111 billion merger violates antitrust laws.

related.title: Paramount and Warner Bros. merger hit with temporary restraining order
related.description: A California judge granted a temporary restraining order as part of the lawsuit brought by state attorneys general due to antitrust concerns.
related.normalizedTitle: paramount and warner bros merger hit with temporary restraining order

articles.title: Zelensky Faces Pressure to Remove Ukraine’s Top General
articles.description: Demonstrators and many soldiers want President Volodymyr Zelensky to reaffirm the vision of drone warfare laid out by the defense minister he fired last week.

articles.title: Oil prices edge higher after Trump says Iran will pay for killing U.S. service members
articles.description: The U.S. has bombed Iran for nine consecutive nights in retaliation for repeated attacks on oil tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: U.S. Average Gas Price Returns to $4 a Gallon as Iran Crisis Escalates
articles.description: A month ago, drivers were enjoying some relief at the pump after the U.S. and Iran signed a deal intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Now, prices are rising again.

articles.title: Deaths of 2 U.S. Troops in Jordan Brings American Toll in the War With Iran to 16
articles.description: President Trump has vowed to avenge the deaths of any U.S. military personnel.

articles.title: Europe's climate agenda risks crushing its industry, environment minister warns
articles.description: Czech Environment Minister Igor Červený warns Europe will "crush" its industrial base unless leaders retreat from aggressive climate policies.

articles.title: Trump Administration Sought Phone Records of Times Journalists and Their Relatives
articles.description: The government is aggressively seeking the confidential sources of Times reporters who wrote about the president’s new Air Force One. The Times has asked a judge to quash the subpoenas.

articles.title: Hamas Names Khalil al-Hayya as Its New Leader
articles.description: Seen as closer to Iran, Khalil al-Hayya served as the Palestinian militant group’s chief negotiator with Israel and the Trump administration during the Gaza war.

articles.title: Micah Lasher, Congressman in Waiting, Has a Few Tricks Up His Sleeve
articles.description: Mr. Lasher, the likely successor to Representative Jerry Nadler, has an unusual back story: As a teenager, he was a prodigy who performed magic tricks on national television.

articles.title: Pentagon Identifies 2 U.S. Soldiers Killed in Jordan During Iran Strike
articles.description: Both were active-duty soldiers, the Defense Department said. They were killed in action after an Iranian strike on a U.S. base on Friday.

articles.title: Pentagon Withheld Dozens of U.S. Military Injuries in Iran War
articles.description: Central Command is not required to release information about injured troops, especially when they quickly return to duty, a military official said.

articles.title: The FDA made a big reversal in its cyclosporiasis probe, but it still thinks iceberg lettuce is to blame
articles.description: The FDA's investigation into the source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak is causing confusion as companies and consumers alike navigate new updates.

articles.title: Another U.S. Service Member Is Killed as Iran War Expands
articles.description: The Pentagon said a service member was killed in northern Iraq while troops disposed of an Iranian drone. It also said “unidentified remains” were found in Jordan, where an earlier attack had left two soldiers dead and another missing.

articles.title: Did This World Cup Change Soccer Forever?
articles.description: With a wartime host nation, a referee deported and a ban overturned after a call from the White House, the 2026 tournament tested FIFA like no other.

articles.title: Man with airsoft rifles caused fireworks explosion outside NYC immigration court: MS NOW
articles.description: The FBI told CNBC that an individual set off "an incendiary device" outside 26 Federal Plaza, which contains a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement office.

articles.title: A.I. ‘Vibecoded’ Apps Are Flooding Apple’s App Store
articles.description: Mobile apps are easy to make with artificial intelligence. That doesn’t mean people are excited to use them.

articles.title: Rubio Says That U.S. Is Still Open to Diplomacy With Iran
articles.description: Iranian foreign ministry officials and Secretary of State Marco Rubio signaled openness to resume negotiations but declined to provide any new details.

articles.title: Oil Markets on Edge as Houthi Rebels Threaten Shipping in the Middle East
articles.description: The global oil price went above $90 but pulled back on signs of hope for a reduction in tensions.

articles.title: Trump says Netanyahu won't be arrested in New York, pushing back on Mamdani
articles.description: Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is the target of an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Criminal Court related to the war on Gaza.

articles.title: Ukraine War Momentum Helps Spur Interest in Postwar Investment
articles.description: At a conference on reconstruction, the focus shifted to private investment, not donor assistance.

articles.title: Greg Sankey addresses SEC breakaway talk as Michigan tries to steal spotlight with AD Warde Manuel decision
articles.description: Greg Sankey opened SEC Media Days addressing self-governance talks as Michigan released its $12 million investigation findings at the same time.

articles.title: Army IDs 2 victims killed in Jordan air base attack; Trump: Iran will pay 'many times over'
articles.description: Two Army soldiers killed at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan are identified as the first U.S. deaths since Iran violated Trump's peace agreement.

articles.title: Tucson mass shooting has residents concerned over ongoing rampant violence, pols debating gun rights
articles.description: Nine people were wounded in a downtown Tucson mass shooting near Empire Pizza before an officer shot the fleeing suspect, who has life-threatening injuries

articles.title: War erupts on far left as DSA endorses challenger against sitting House progressive
articles.description: The Democratic Socialists of America is backing Donavan McKinney as they target progressive Rep. Shri Thanedar in Michigan's primary.

articles.title: Mamdani’s ‘deranged’ Israel take resurfaces as he targets Netanyahu
articles.description: A resurfaced video clip shows Mayor Zoran Mamdani calling Israel "an idea" rather than a country, sparking fierce social media backlash and criticism.

articles.title: What to know about testosterone replacement therapy as the U.S. military plans to test troops
articles.description: While some research has shown benefits for patients who qualify, questions remain about the therapy's long-term risks and use among men without a medical need.

articles.title: Michigan candidate Abdul El-Sayed dodges when pressed on controversial remarks about death of Khamenei
articles.description: Abdul El-Sayed dodged Jake Tapper's questions about remarks on Dearborn residents being saddened by Ali Khamenei's death during the U.S.-Iran conflict.

articles.title: RedHook Android malware can quietly hijack your phone
articles.description: RedHook Android malware abuses wireless debugging and accessibility permissions to gain shell-level control after victims sideload a malicious APK.

articles.title: Minnesota of the Caribbean: Trump admin freezes vacation hotspot’s funds over alleged hurricane-aid abuses
articles.description: HUD suspended the Virgin Islands Housing Finance Authority from federal funding over alleged fraud and mismanagement of hurricane relief funds.

articles.title: Ryanair says 'no shortage' of travelers despite Iran war denting profit
articles.description: Ryanair's first-quarter profit took a 34% hit as consumers delayed bookings due to the Middle East crisis, while struggling airlines face a "difficult winter."

articles.title: Houthis in Yemen Declare Red Sea Blockade on Saudi Arabia
articles.description: The Iran-backed Houthi militia, which controls parts of Yemen, has in the past blockaded the Red Sea by threatening a choke point crucial to Saudi oil exports and global shipping.

articles.title: MTG pounces on Hegseth, claiming US troop casualties 'fuel' his 'bloodlust for a senseless war'
articles.description: Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene accused War Secretary Pete Hegseth of "bloodlust" in response to a post Hegseth issued about fallen U.S. service members.

articles.title: North Sea Drilling Offers First Test for Burnham’s Relationship With Trump
articles.description: Andy Burnham has not yet met President Trump, but the two men will be expected to speak shortly after Mr. Burnham becomes prime minister.

articles.title: Earthquake in Peru Kills 6 and Displaces Hundreds
articles.description: Peru is prone to frequent quakes, but the 5.5-magnitude temblor was the biggest to strike the central highlands area in more than 50 years.

articles.title: Man sets off device at federal building in New York, triggering terrorism probe
articles.description: In a statement, the FBI’s New York field office said “an individual had deployed an incendiary device” outside 26 Federal Plaza and “was taken into custody.”

articles.title: Young people prefer to watch than join when it comes to politics
articles.description: A poll found that nearly half of young people under age 30 prefer to engage politically by liking or sharing news content online.

articles.title: A religious sect is at the heart of an old fight getting attention in a GOP race

articles.title: Coinbase trading results may fall short of expectations, according to prediction markets

articles.title: 'Give me my money back': South Korean traders' leveraged SK Hynix, Samsung bets unravel after sell-off

articles.title: TSMC is accelerating Arizona factory build-out to capitalize on AI 'megatrend,' CFO says

articles.title: Bezos backs CuspAI as startup teams up with Nvidia to hunt for chipmaking materials
articles.description: CuspAI is part of a new wave of companies looking to use AI to solve challenges in the physical world.

articles.title: China's car market heads for worst year since 2021 as sales plunge 20%
articles.description: Consumer demand for cars in China tumbles following record-high sales of 23.7 million units in 2025.

articles.title: Samsung Biologics makes $1.8 billion all-cash offer for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group
articles.description: South Korea's Samsung Biologics said on Monday that it had made an all-cash bid of 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion) for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group.

articles.description: Markets are underestimating the impact of climate volatility on a range of asset classes, commodity strategists say.

articles.title: FIFA emerges as the $9 billion winner as the biggest, brashest World Cup ever draws to a close
articles.description: The football governing body presided over the most lucrative sporting event in history and is poised to net over $9 billion in revenue for 2026.
